title: Full Mock Interview Simulation
learningObjectives: Apply **full mock interview simulation** with production-grade tests, tools, and measurable outcomes
Explain trade-offs between test types: unit, slice, integration, contract, e2e
Use profiling and benchmarking vocabulary correctly in senior interviews
Deliver behavioral answers with STAR structure tied to real architecture decisions
Run mock interviews with follow-up depth on testing and performance
Connect reliability practices from earlier phases to capstone scenarios

sections.title: Why Full Mock Interview Simulation matters
sections.content: The **full** **round** **simulation** builds **muscle** **memory** for **pressure**, **time**, and **follow-ups**—the **difference** between **knowing** **topics** and **passing** **panels**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Full Mock Interview Simulation
sections.content:
### Day 90 — **Mock** **interview** **simulation**

### 1. Cadence
**Warmup**, **deep** **dive**, **retro**.

### 2. Communication
**Think** **aloud**, **confirm** **assumptions**.

### 3. Depth
**Interviewer** **steers**—adapt **quickly**.

### 4. Bodies of work
**Java**, **Spring**, **distributed** **systems**, **design**.

### 5. Mindset
**Curiosity** and **calm** **recovery** from **mistakes**.

### 6. Story
**Post-mock** **note**: **one** **diagram** **skill** **and** **one** **Java** **API** **drill** **for** **tomorrow**.

sections.title: Basic — Session structure
sections.output: intro -> coding or design -> behavioral -> your questions

sections.title: Intermediate — Think-aloud habit
sections.output: narrate assumptions before silent coding tunnel

sections.title: Advanced — Follow-up recovery
sections.output: if stumped: narrow scope, propose experiment, offer trade-off table

sections.title: Exercise — Full Mock Interview Simulation (Day 90 assignment)
sections.problem:
Print **exactly** two lines:
1. Implemented: Full Mock Interview Simulation
2. Focus: Mixed full-round simulation with follow-up probing.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: How to **timebox** **design** **section**?
conceptual.answer: 5 clarify, 10 high-level, 10 deep dive, 5 wrap—adjust to interviewer pace. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Mock interview simulation**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. 5 clarify, 10 high-level, 10 deep dive, 5 wrap—adjust to interviewer pace. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. 5 clarify, 10 high-level, 10 deep dive, 5 wrap—adjust to interviewer pace. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.question: **Follow-up** **probing** **strategy**?
conceptual.answer: Pause, restate assumption, answer narrow question, reconnect to diagram. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

**(4) Prevention** Add **canary** gate and **diff** **alerts** on **golden** metrics. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Skill | Drill |
|---|---|
| Timebox | visible clock |
| Clarify | ask early |
| Diagram | legible boxes |
| Trade-off | 3 options |
| Recover | breathe restate |
| Code | tests aloud |
| Behavioral | STAR metric |
| End | smart questions |
| Retro | one fix |
| Repeat | weekly |
